title: rubus corylifolius subsp. acuminatus
intro: Rubus corylifolius subsp. acuminatus is a subspecies of the Rubus corylifolius plant, commonly known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan strawberry. It is a type of thorny shrub native to the Himalayas and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients, offering various health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Rubus corylifolius subsp. acuminatus can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine preparations, including powders, capsules, and ointments.
